question: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by end of 2026?

rawPayload.question: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by end of 2026?
rawPayload.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires that constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires that only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

events.title: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by end of 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires that constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires that only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects skepticism over a full Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by December 31, 2026, driven by ongoing Russian offensive operations in areas like northern Sumy Oblast as of April 12 and the absence of breakthroughs in recent U.S.-mediated peace talks. Although a temporary Orthodox Easter truce was agreed last week and reports emerged April 10 of parties moving toward a potential deal, earlier negotiations in Geneva and Abu Dhabi stalled amid the U.S.-Israel-Iran war, only resuming hopes after Iran's April 8 ceasefire. Zelenskyy has signaled openness to halting attacks if Russia ceases energy infrastructure strikes, but entrenched demands over territory, security guarantees, and sanctions persist amid battlefield stalemates, with no major de-escalation signals in the past 30 days. Upcoming talks could shift dynamics, yet historical patterns of protracted conflicts weigh against resolution this year.
